params.name: Plants & Birds
params.description: Plants & Birds is a work by Bian Jingshao from the Ming dynasty. The painting is made of a pine tree, a bamboo, a plum tree, and rocks, dotted with birds, a metaphor for the officials in the imperial court. The birds have different postures, looking vivid and animated, demonstrating the painter's pursuit of incorruptibility and harmony. It was collected by the imperial palace right after its completion, and was passed down to later generations as a valuable cultural relic!
